strengths: Direct production experience with agentic-AI infrastructure (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) at re:cinq, aligning with the CTO role's requirement for modular event-driven multi-agent frameworks

Proven high-scale distributed systems expertise (handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby, real-time metadata systems) and observability rigor (CI/CD, GitLab infrastructure at Altom Consulting)

Strong fintech-adjacent exposure through pricing optimization at Optilyz, demonstrating quantitative decisioning and commercial impact, plus open-source GIS platform governance (GISCollective) showing event-driven architecture and Kubernetes deployment
concerns: Limited explicit fintech-infrastructure experience (e.g., credit systems, payment flows) though pricing optimization at Optilyz provides adjacent financial modeling exposure
No direct regulatory/compliance track record mentioned, which is critical for the CTO role in fintech
Minimal hands-on experience with hybrid engineering governance models (external delivery partners) despite dev-ops infrastructure background—needs verification of governance frameworks
response.justification: Bogdan’s resume demonstrates a rare combination of agentic-AI production work (MCP frameworks, orchestration) and multi-scale distributed systems leadership (millions of requests/sec, real-time metadata, Kubernetes). While fintech specifics are lighter, his pricing optimization at Optilyz and event-driven GISCollective platform show quantitative, high-stakes decisioning and architectural rigor. His dev-ops and CI/CD depth (GitLab, trunk-based development) positions him well to build governance and observability for regulated AI systems, making him a compelling candidate despite some fintech regulatory gaps.
